title: ₹14.65 lakh salary? Here's how you can legally reduce your income tax liability to zero under new tax regime
summary: A ₹14.65 lakh CTC does not necessarily mean you have to pay income tax under the new tax regime. With the right salary structure, employer contributions to EPF and NPS, and the standard deduction, your taxable income can be brought down to ₹11.99 lakh, making you eligible for zero tax liability.

title: Mutual funds holding cash: Is high cash balance a red flag for investors? Experts explain
summary: Experts suggest that cash holdings should be viewed as a portfolio management tool rather than a buy-or-sell signal. A fund's investment philosophy, portfolio quality, and long-term performance remain far more important than a single percentage shown in the monthly factsheet.

title: EPF vs Labour Codes: If basic pay must be 50%, why is PF capped at ₹1,800?
summary: Payroll rules are changing in India as EPF vs labour codes reshape salary structures and PF calculations. While basic pay norms rise, EPF contributions remain capped at ₹1,800, impacting take-home pay and retirement planning choices.

title: Gold drops 30%, silver plunges 54% — but history shows 2026 correction isn’t the deepest yet
summary: Gold and silver have fallen sharply from their record highs in 2026, with gold down about 30% and silver by 54%. However, historical cycles show the current declines are not the deepest, and the time correction is still pending.&nbsp;

title: EPFO's ₹1,800 EPF contribution cap explained: Who benefits more – employees or employers?
summary: The EPF Scheme, 2026 introduces clearer guidelines on provident fund contributions, capping mandatory payments at ₹1,800 monthly. While employees benefit from increased take-home pay and flexibility, they may risk a smaller retirement fund if they don't opt for additional voluntary contributions.

title: Why balanced hybrid funds are back in focus
summary: A balanced hybrid strategy aims to keep the equity-debt allocation closer to a 50:50 mix, providing more stability and limiting downside risk.
